ead.archdesc:
======= RMM04082Price family papers4082Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
March 20102B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
geneo naclThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:46:55 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Price family papers
Price family.
Price, David, 1940-1999. (Title of work: Traditional and transformational analysis of Sherente kinship terminology..)
4082
15.5 cubic feet. 15.5 cubic feet.
1881-1982. Audio Recordings, Correspondence, Diaries, Manuscripts, Photographs, Research Materials Published and unpublished stories and articles by Edgar Price and genealogical material concerning the Price family. Correspondence from between family members. Typescript field notes by David Price on Nambiquara (Satones) Indian groups in Brazil. Notes, tape recordings, memos, published and unpublished reports used to prepare book about Nambicuara Indians of Brazil; and autobiographical writing by David Price. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 5 6 7 8 10 11 18 22 23 24 25 28 2 3 4 9 12 13 14 15 16 17 19 20 21 26 27 28 1+ ======= 1 5 6 7 8 10 11 18 22 23 24 25 28 2 3 4 9 12 13 14 15 16 17 19 20 21 26 27 28 1+ >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2070621
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
Various
EAD encoding:
Evan Fay Earle, March 2010
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, diaries, travel albums and journals, accounts, financial and legal papers, post cards and photographs, published and unpublished stories and articles by Edgar Price, advertising copy and other writing, and genealogical material concerning the Price family, primarily Edgar Dayton Price, Paul D. Price, Arthur B. Price, Francis Smith Price (Mrs. Edgar D.), and Florence Price Beeson, and including letters and a silk color sample book from her dressmaking establishment in Shanghai, China.
Also, correspondence from Paul and Hazel Price to David Price, 1943-1982; correspondence, notes, notebooks, population data, and other material written by David Price in the 1950s and typescript field notes by David Price on Nambiquara (Satones) (Nambicuara) Indian groups in Brazil, 1967-1970. Notes, tape recordings, memos, and published and unpublished reports used to prepare his book about Nambiquara; and autobiographical writing by David Price,1998.
Also, Master's thesis that David Price wrote at the University of Chicago, "A Traditional and Transformational Analysis of Sherente Kinship Terminology," 1967, Bd. Ms. 1.
Cite As:
Price family papers, #4082. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Genealogies. Brazil -- Description and travel. China -- Description and travel. Indians of South America -- Brazil. Dressmaking -- China. Voyages and travels. Advertising. Families. Huichol Indians. Nambicuara Indians -- Social conditions. Beeson, Florence Price. Price, Arthur B. Price, David, 1940-1999. Price, Edgar Dayton. Price, Francis Smith. Price, Hazel. Price, Paul D.

Scope and Contents
Photographs of native people, village, huts, camp, including some aerial photos showing deforestation of the Guaporé Valley, Mato Grosso. Zillo Ranch by Pedro Agostinho, Federal University of Bahia.Maps of the Guaporé Valley208Thesis, "History and Survival of the Xavante Indians of Central Brazil" by Nancy M. Flowers, Hunter College209

Scope and Contents
Photographs of native people, village, huts, camp, including some aerial photos showing deforestation of the Guaporé Valley, Mato Grosso. Zillo Ranch by Pedro Agostinho, Federal University of Bahia.Maps of the Guaporé Valley208Thesis, "History and Survival of the Xavante Indians of Central Brazil" by Nancy M. Flowers, Hunter College209
